4	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
RESEARCH EXAMPLE
HIGH TICKET COACHING
JOEY PERCIA
I. WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR…
INSIGHTS INTO DEMOGRAPHIC
Who Is Your Customer?
• 	Coach/consultant/service providers wanting to sell more higher-ticket
programs and packages
• 	B2B - business to business. Financial ROI offers
• 	Making 4-15k+ /month
• 	Sales/enrollment is their bottleneck
What Attitudes Do They Have? (Religious, Political, Social, Economic)?
• 	The market is rigged against them
• 	Hard to make the economics work and pay to run to ads
• 	People don’t want to be pitched or “sold” on social media.
What Are Their Hopes and Dreams?
• 	Step-By-Step sales process to follow
° Don’t want to sound like a robot or fumble over words trying to follow
a script
• 	Ready-to-Buy Clients…pre-sold
• 	Make More Money While Making a Difference
• 	Negotiating better deals
° Higher pay, less work, coaching clients are less demanding
• 	Instead of 500-2k/month and people cancel -- enroll clients into 3, 6, even
12 months packages.
• 	Sign up 5 high paying packages each week, rather than 10 of the smaller
packages.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 17 --

5	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
• 	Unlimited leads of people who’re perfect fits
• 	Financial Freedom —
° Stop trading your dollars for hours
• 	Know exactly what to say to get people who have no idea who you are to
ask about your coaching in 10-15 words
• 	Know exactly what to say to open sales conversations without it feeling
awkward.
• 	Have more control of their life
• 	Build a mid 6-figure coaching business with low stress
• 	Bigger paydays
• 	Take their family on more vacations
• 	Work with “higher end” clients
• 	Increase your price by a factor of 2x, 5x, even 10x…while working for better
clients on better projects
• 	ongoing 	stream 	of 	qualified 	leads 	using 	street-tested, 	step-by-step
inbound and outbound marketing strategies
• 	grow your revenue from 5 to 6 figures…even up to 7 figures… using proven
strategies and systems designed for high-powered consultants only
• 	leapfrog over your competitors and advance from being a good technician
to a sought-after professional
• 	get paid what you deserve…
° even if you don’t know how to ask for it.
° Even if you don’t have 15 years of experience
° Even in this economy...when they “don’t have a budget”
• 	consistently land 5-figure raises
What Are Their Victories and Failures?
VICTORIES
• 	I no longer struggle to close clients at $180 to $200/month, despite the
fact they offered amazing service.
• 	2 months after starting my coaching business, I finally booked 3 paying
coaching clients.
• 	The first proposal we sent out won us a new client.
• 	Hit a record high with new client enrollment
• 	Sell in a way that fits r personality
• 	Signed up a new coaching client, and it didn’t involve any unproductive
cold-calling.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

6	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
• 	Now I get high quality clients via Facebook, IG, and LinkedIn
• 	Consistently sign up coaching clients instead of during the peak times of
the year.
• 	The first three dream accounts granted me appointments. I struggled for
a year or more in some cases to get in front of them.
• 	I am confident and comfortable asking for the sale/quoting my packages
• 	Have a better relationship with my fiancée by actively listening to her
before jointly finding solutions
• 	Won heated conversations with parents and friends
• 	Brought angry customers — who felt we had failed them — back from the
brink to trusting us again
• 	Forged a better relationship with my business partners by understanding
how they value time, silence, relationships, surprises, etc…
• 	Gotten discounts on things that I didn’t think could be discounted, just by
using my name
• 	Gotten to the front of the waiting line at busy restaurants
• 	Said no to bad deals, because no deal is better than a bad one
• 	Earn potential clients’ trust to build a lasting relationship
• 	This week, I was going to purchase a used car that I had seen on the
internet. The car was priced right, but they were adding a $699 doc fee
to the price of the car, and the car was only $6K. Something made me
think of the book, and I went and got it off the bookshelf. I went to the
index in the back of the book and there were a few pages on buying a
car. I read them and felt empowered. I went into the dealership the next
day and bought the car for $900 less than their original “Out the door”
price that they “just couldn’t go down on”. I used all the tactics in the few
pages I had read. I couldn’t believe how the whole deal unfolded just like
I had read in the book. It was amazing and I was sooooo proud of myself!
Now I am going to read the whole book and learn a whole lot more about
dealing with people in everyday situations!! I would recommend this book
to everyone!!! It just saved me $900, so the $11 investment was more
than worth it! I wish I could meet the author in person. I am so impressed
with everything in the book!
• 	Saved a full 7% or $4,000 on car of my dreams since I was a boy!!! Best
book you’ll ever buy!
• 	Get kids to do chores when you want them to?
• 	How much confidence will you have going into your next business deal
knowing the secrets and (dare I say “hacks”) on negotiating the best deal
possible?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

7	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
• 	What would it be like to look forward to negotiations and think of them as
fun instead of intimidating?
• 	I’m making 3-4x my salary as an X-ray tech.
• 	“There was this glass ceiling that Erin helped me see through and now I’m
having $40,000 months...” - Justin
• 	earning $3,000 to $10,000 per client within 24 to 48 hours of them clicking
on your ad or opting onto your list, even if they’ve near heard of you before
• 	I sold my first high ticket offer at $3,000. From there I went up to sell even
higher ticket services. The second biggest high-ticket sale I made then
was for $9,000. And the third highest priced offer I sold was for $15,000.
FAILURES
• 	Ups and downs in leads
• 	No steady income - I’m starting from scratch every month
• 	Afraid to hire because I don’t have consistent revenue coming in.
• 	Trained to do your job―not sell it.
• 	Feel a bit lost, hesitant, or ‘behind’ on sales calls
° I always feel like I’m doing it wrong
° I either under- or over-selling.
• 	I talked myself out of an 8k sales
• 	Got rejected 9 out of the last 10 calls
• 	People already tell me they can’t afford it… how would I raise my fees?
• 	I went back and asked a client why they sign up and they said it was
because they felt like I was begging for their business/
° I’m terrified this is how I sound all the time now.
° I can’t get out of my own head.
• 	I’ve had 4-5 mental breakdowns. I’m not cut out for this
• 	I had an 8k month and then, only make 1k for the next 4 months
• 	She took money out on credit cards, sold belongings, and put her back
completely against the wall.
• 	But I knew that if I kept charging $800 per client, I would wear myself out.
• 	Late at night, when the house was quiet and everyone else was asleep,
I was awake scrolling my phone, looking for a solution to turn my baby
coaching business into a full-time income.
• 	I kept seeing the stories of brand-new coaches immediately having a five-
figure launch or making six-figures in a year and I couldn’t figure out what
they were doing differently than me.
• 	Why was everyone else earning five-figures (or more) per month online
and I couldn’t seem to make it work?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

8	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
• 	I could book one on one clients here and there, but I was quickly burning
myself out because I felt like I had to keep chasing new clients.
• 	Needless to say, that the $15,000 sale didn’t pan out well. This was
because I couldn’t deliver on my promise and it was HARD! The sale
was for a consulting service where I promised to help the guy build a
marketing system that will help him get to at least $10K per month in the
first 90 days, selling his ecom store creation service. The problem was
that I didn’t know that it will take him much longer to get to get him to
this goal. To cut a long story short, I gave him a refund, and also gave him
$1500 on top of it as a compensation for wasting his time - with lots of
apologies to boot.
• 	I felt devastated. I couldn’t sleep well at night. Not because I lost the
money, but because someone just LOST FAITH in me...and to me, that’s
the worst feeling in the world - disappointing someone.
• 	I kept sending this guy resources and guidance even after the refund and
stuff, just to help him make that dream a reality. Because when this man
came to me, I gave him lots of hope before he could sign up to my $15,000
consulting service.
• 	Needless to say, that months later, he made his first $10,000 building
out ecom stores for people - using the SAME system I built for him. From
there he hired a team and went on to build a multiple 6-figure business.
Because of my determination to help this guy succeed at ALL COST, till
date, he emails me occasionally to say thank you.
What Outside Forces Do THEY Believe Have Prevented Their Best Life?
• 	I had no audience.
• 	I had little experience (or so I thought).
• 	I had no email list (unless you count those 6 people).
• 	I had no clue.
• 	All the competition
• 	Market is saturated
• 	How do I compete with bigger businesses?
° Running ads and have teams
• 	Objections
° Objection #6 – I’m happy with what I have right now
° Objection #5: “I need to think about it.”
° Objection #4: “I’ve had a bad experience with this product or brand in
the past.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

9	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
° Objection #3: I need to consult with my significant other first.”
° Objection #2: “Isn’t this cheaper online?”
° Objection #1: “It’s too expensive.”
What Are Their Prejudices?
• 	Don’t want to be seen as salesy
Sum Up Their Core Beliefs About Life, Love, and Family In 1-3 Sentences.
• 	They want to make an honest living doing what they enjoy, and they want
to provide a safe, secure, and stable life for their family.
OTHER EXISTING SOLUTIONS
What is the Market Already Using? (List Out)
• 	Build a modern, professional website
• 	Blog 7 days a week
• 	Be active on Facebook
• 	Join Twitter
• 	Sharing Images on Instagram and Pinterest
• 	Making Videos
• 	Do a Podcast
• 	Creating Infographics
• 	Building Attractive Landing Pages
• 	Offer Free Gifts
• 	Email Marketing
What Has Their Experience Been Like?
• 	Inconsistent income
• 	Haven’t been able to get steady clients
° Some months are way better than others
° Feel like they’re barely keeping their head above water
• 	Don’t want to increase rates, they don’t know how many more leads they’re
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 17 --

10	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
What Does the Market LIKE About Existing Solutions?
• 	They enjoy coaching
• 	This is what they want to do
• 	They’re making money, just not enough — or as much as they want to
What Does the Market DISLIKE About Existing Solutions?
• 	Hasn’t worked
• 	Takes a lot of time
• 	Feels in inauthentic to them
• 	They feel sleazy
• 	Want to come from a more helpful place not a hardcore “closer”
Are There Horror Stories About Existing Solutions?
• 	I spent well over $20,000 on coaches and courses; investing almost all of
my savings to make it work.
• 	I felt paralyzed to take more action because I couldn’t be sure I wasn’t
just wasting more money
• 	...boosted posts to get discovery calls booked but all I did was waste
more money on Facebook™ ads that provided no return
• 	...hosted live stream after live stream which only drained my introvert
energy and kept me chained to my desk
• 	...gave away free calls hoping to turn those calls into clients but most of
the calls ended with “I’ll get back to you.”
• 	...posted in Facebook groups spending hours and hours “providing value”
instead of doing what I really wanted to do, which was actually work with
clients.
Does the Market Believe Existing Solution Works?
• 	Yes, but they’re sick of trying coaching programs and courses without
getting the results they’re searching.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 17 --

11	Stefan Georgi
MODULE 2	Research Example - High Ticket Coaching Joey Percia
CURIOSITY
Has Someone Tried to Solve the Market’s Pain Points Before In A Very
Unique Way?
• 	Courses, but nothing really unique or new.
What Was The Result?
• 	Sales here or there.
• 	Didn’t stick to it because it felt inauthentic. Too salesy. Was too time-
consuming.
Is There A Conspiratorial Story Behind Why Old Solutions Didn’t Work?
No.
Are There Any Older Attempts to Solve the Problem (Pre-1960) That Are
Unique?
• 	Sales books going back for ages.
• 	Feels like all the material is the same.
• 	Something is missing they just don’t know what.
What Happened? Were they successful but forgotten? Or were they a
failure? Why?
• 	Everything they went through has them coming from a hard sales position.
• 	They want to serve.
• 	They want to grow and help more people.
• 	They want to be a leader, not a pushy salesman
CORRUPTION
Is There A Belief That the Market’s Pain Point USED To Not Exist, Or Used
To Not Be So Bad?
• 	Other marketers and coaches are saturating the market and people don’t
trust them anymore.
• 	They didn’t deliver on their promises now people think they won’t deliver.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
